response.justification: The candidate is a strong, versatile senior engineer with 15+ years of experience, solid systems-level work in DLang, and proven mentoring ability, but the role demands deep, current hands-on C++ expertise for industrial automation. Their C/C++ experience is limited to one early-career role and a thesis project, with no evidence of MQTT, message-broker architectures at scale in C++, IEC 62443, or SDL practices. The profile skews heavily toward web/full-stack TypeScript and Node.js, making the fit for this C++-centric industrial position weak despite overall seniority.
strengths: Extensive overall engineering experience across embedded, desktop, web, and low-latency systems
Systems-programming depth in DLang (async I/O, fibers, high-throughput microservices) transferable to C++ concepts
Strong mentoring, TDD, and craftsmanship track record aligned with the role's coaching expectations
concerns: No recent professional C++ work; last dedicated C/C++ role appears to be early in career
No exposure to industrial cybersecurity standards (IEC 62443), SDL, or protocols like MQTT, OPC UA, Modbus
Career trajectory is web/full-stack focused, suggesting misalignment with an industrial automation C++ role
